message:
fix(sync): refuse to auto-flush or silently overwrite a JSONL that still holds merge-conflict markers

Previously, post-command auto-flush called `export_to_jsonl_with_policy`
unconditionally, which rewrote `.beads/issues.jsonl` from the live DB on
every mutating CLI command. If the working tree contained an unresolved
`git merge` on the JSONL (`<<<<<<<` / `=======` / `>>>>>>>` regions), that
export silently clobbered both the local and remote sides of the merge.

Auto-import's conflict-markers scan caught this on the read path, but any
command invoked with `--no-auto-import` bypassed it entirely, leaving the
auto-flush write path as the last line of defense.

Teach `auto_flush()` to detect the same markers before touching the JSONL:
when `ensure_no_conflict_markers(jsonl_path)` fails, log at warn level and
return `AutoFlushResult::default()`. The mutation still lands in the DB —
the in-memory state is correct — but the on-disk JSONL keeps its
unresolved merge until the operator runs `br sync --flush-only --force`
or clears the markers by hand.

tests/e2e_errors.rs: add two full end-to-end regressions:

- `e2e_auto_flush_skips_silently_overwriting_conflict_markered_jsonl`
  seeds a valid JSONL, wraps it in `<<<<<<<` / `=======` / `>>>>>>>`,
  runs a mutating command with `--no-auto-import` (so auto-import's guard
  is off), then asserts the mutation succeeds AND the on-disk JSONL is
  still byte-for-byte identical to the conflicted content.

- `e2e_sync_flush_checks_conflict_markers_before_noop_short_circuit`
  pins the explicit-flush path: `br sync --flush-only` used to no-op
  when nothing was dirty, which could hide unresolved JSONL conflicts
  even from users running sync for safety. The new test asserts the
  command surfaces the conflict-marker error instead of short-circuiting
  silently.

Small rustfmt wrapping cleanups in the same file are bundled with the
substantive change.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
